pr.title: Match Ninja flags more closely to Make in CI
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated the GitHub Actions Ninja build path so its configure flags align more closely with the Make-based CI path. The workflow now passes compiler path, max error, verbose, and version flags in a reordered configure argument string, and adds --require-protos when the default mapped build is used.

what_changed.change: Changed the CI Build step configure args from '--version ${{matrix.version}} --compilers /compilers' to '--compilers /compilers --max-errors 0 --verbose --version ${{matrix.version}}'.
When matrix.options is empty, changed the mapped build configure args from '--map $args' to '--map --require-protos $args'.
Kept the execution model the same: configure.py receives args through xargs, then ninja is invoked with matrix.options.

smart_moves: Aligned Ninja CI behavior with the Make path using flags rather than changing build scripts or source code.
Added --require-protos to the default mapped Ninja build, making CI stricter about prototype availability when applicable.
Added --max-errors 0 and --verbose to configure invocation, which should make CI failures more complete and diagnosable.
Limited the change to a two-line workflow adjustment, reducing risk while tightening CI parity.
decomp_lessons: CI paths that are intended to validate equivalent builds should use equivalent configure flags; otherwise Ninja-only or Make-only behavior can hide decomp issues.
For decompilation projects, stricter CI flags such as --require-protos can catch header/prototype drift earlier.
Verbose configure/build output and unlimited error reporting are useful in CI because they preserve more evidence for matching and build failures.
Small workflow flag changes can materially improve trust in automated matching checks without touching decompiled code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic was present in the diff.
The relevant matching-adjacent tactic is CI parity: make the Ninja path enforce the same or closer validation constraints as the Make path.
searchable_terms: Match Ninja flags more closely to Make in CI
follow_up_queries: Search for other CI build paths that invoke configure.py without --require-protos.
Compare Make and Ninja CI configure flags after PR 1278 for remaining differences.
Look for later PRs that adjust --max-errors, --verbose, or --require-protos in build.yml.
